M-049.NAME: mtproto-live-edge-runtime
M-049.path: mtproto-runtime backend/app/mtproto/runtime_bridge.py backend/app/mtproto/provisioning.py nginx/nginx.conf docker-compose.yml deploy/deploy-on-server.sh deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml scripts/phase37-mtproto-runtime-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-37.xml docs/plans/Phase-38.xml docs/plans/Phase-41.xml
M-049.owns: Live KPprotoN/mtproto_proxy runtime, token-protected policy API, DE MTProto data-plane ownership, and HTTPS domain-fronting fallback.
M-049.purpose: Make issued KrotPN MTProto assignments reachable by Telegram clients by running a real fake-TLS MTProto edge instead of only generating proxy links.

exports.runtime-mtproto-edge: Host-network mtproto-edge service owns public TCP 443 and loads KPprotoN-compatible fake-TLS policy.
exports.runtime-policy-api: Local HTTP policy API applies, revokes, and health-checks issued SNI domains using a deploy-generated bearer token.
exports.https-fallback: Normal HTTPS requests are domain-fronted from the MTProto edge to the configured private frontend fallback.
exports.provisioning-live-apply: Backend provisioning applies an issued SNI to the live edge before returning an active proxy payload.
exports.runtime-smoke: Phase-37 static and local runtime smoke checks protect service wiring, nginx port ownership, and secret redaction.
exports.telegram-upstream-routes: Deploy keeps Telegram MTProto upstream ranges routed through the existing RU-to-DE awg-client tunnel so RU edge can reach Telegram DCs without changing the host default route.
exports.phase38-supersession: Phase-38 keeps the runtime artifacts but moves production traffic to a DE-hosted runtime behind an RU SNI router.

runtime-policy.policy-1: Public TCP 443 has exactly one runtime owner: mtproto-edge. Nginx serves HTTPS fallback on internal 8443.
runtime-policy.policy-2: The edge must accept issued SNI policies only through the localhost policy API with MTPROTO_RUNTIME_TOKEN.
runtime-policy.policy-3: Existing MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX and MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT values must be preserved across hotfix deploys.
runtime-policy.policy-4: Normal HTTPS, /api, and /admin traffic must continue working through the fallback path after mtproto-edge starts.
runtime-policy.policy-5: Logs and health payloads may expose status and counts, but never base secret, salt, derived secrets, full tg:// links, private keys, JWTs, or email tokens.
runtime-policy.policy-6: MTProto bootstrap must use bounded Telegram core fetches with static fallback artifacts so the edge can start when core.telegram.org is slow or blocked.
runtime-policy.policy-7: RU host routes for Telegram upstream ranges must be scoped to Telegram networks only and use awg-client, not a broad default-route mutation.

constraints.constraint-1: Host-network public 443 changes are production-sensitive and must be paired with rollback-ready nginx fallback validation.
constraints.constraint-2: Policy API must bind to localhost only and reject missing or wrong tokens.
constraints.constraint-3: Telegram availability cannot be fully proven by local unit tests; final proof requires a real Telegram client or protocol-compatible live smoke.
constraints.constraint-4: Phase-38 must preserve Phase-37 local runtime work while avoiding RU-only live handshakes as the production dependency.
constraints.constraint-5: Phase-41 must reconcile runtime behavior against CodexKPprotoN_for_KPN before claiming fake-TLS production readiness.

phase-41-plan.step-1: Compare mtproto-runtime fake-TLS, per-SNI policy, and bootstrap paths with the reference CodexKPprotoN_for_KPN implementation.
phase-41-plan.step-2: Keep the DE runtime as the production MTProto listener and apply user SNI policy without official secret manifests.
phase-41-plan.step-3: Expose only redacted health, policy counts, and safe failure codes to KrotPN diagnostics.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-18: Local Erlang compile passed for mtproto-runtime with the token-protected policy handler.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-18: Local Erlang prod release passed from an ASCII temp path; direct release from the Cyrillic checkout path hit a rebar3 path-formatting bug only.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-18: Backend regression tests passed for HTTP policy adapter, provisioning live apply, API payloads, and existing local bridge behavior.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: Live RU application exposed Telegram upstream reachability problems; deploy now routes Telegram MTProto ranges through awg-client and runtime includes bounded static bootstrap fallback before final Telegram Desktop proof.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: RU HTTPS was rolled back to nginx public 443 after mtproto_proxy continued failing downstream handshakes; website remains available while MTProto live proof is blocked.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 plan supersedes RU-only runtime ownership with a DE-backed runtime and RU SNI router, while preserving existing assignments and secrets.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local execution keeps the reusable mtproto-runtime and policy API but moves the local mtproto-edge compose service behind an explicit profile so default public 443 belongs to the RU SNI router.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design selects the KPprotoN fake-TLS runtime as the production data-plane to match the reference project and avoid official MTProxy process restarts on per-user issuance.
evidence.evidence-9: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 local execution passed Erlang compile and static smoke proving the production DE runtime path uses KPprotoN policy apply instead of official secret manifests.

M-049.hotspot: H-036: MTProto live edge must own public TCP 443, forward normal HTTPS to nginx 8443, and load issued SNI policies without rotating existing secrets.
